tasks.title: Initialize Project Scaffolding with Vite + React + TypeScript
tasks.description: Set up the foundational project structure using Vite as the build tool with React 18+ and TypeScript 5+. Configure ESLint, Prettier, and project-specific TypeScript settings.
tasks.details:
1. Run `npm create vite@latest mira-ui -- --template react-ts`
2. Configure `tsconfig.json` with strict mode, path aliases (@/components, @/lib, @/hooks, @/store)
3. Set up ESLint with `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`, React hooks rules
4. Configure Prettier with single quotes, no semicolons, 2-space tabs
5. Create folder structure:
   - `src/components/` (UI components)
   - `src/panels/` (panel types: terminal, kanban, companion)
   - `src/store/` (Zustand stores)
   - `src/hooks/` (custom hooks)
   - `src/lib/` (utilities)
   - `src/types/` (TypeScript interfaces)
6. Install core dependencies: `react`, `react-dom`, `zustand`, `@tanstack/react-query`
7. Create `.mira/` directory scaffold in project root with empty YAML files as placeholders
tasks.testStrategy: Verify `npm run dev` starts dev server. Verify TypeScript compilation succeeds with `npm run build`. ESLint should pass with no errors on initial scaffold.

subtasks.title: Create Vite Project with React-TypeScript Template
subtasks.description: Initialize the Mira Studio frontend project using Vite's official React-TypeScript template to establish the foundation for the UI application.
subtasks.details: Run `npm create vite@latest mira-ui -- --template react-ts` in the project root. This creates a new directory `mira-ui/` with React 18+, TypeScript 5+, and Vite preconfigured. After creation, navigate into the directory and run `npm install` to install base dependencies. Verify the scaffold works by running `npm run dev` and confirming the default Vite React page loads at localhost:5173. The template includes a basic `tsconfig.json`, `vite.config.ts`, and starter `src/` structure that subsequent subtasks will customize.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run dev` and verify the development server starts successfully. Open http://localhost:5173 in a browser and confirm the Vite + React starter page renders. Run `npm run build` to verify TypeScript compilation succeeds with no errors.

subtasks.title: Configure TypeScript with Strict Mode and Path Aliases
subtasks.description: Customize tsconfig.json with strict type checking enabled and configure path aliases for clean imports across the codebase.
subtasks.details: Modify `tsconfig.json` to enable strict mode (`strict: true`) and add path aliases under `compilerOptions.paths`: `@/components/*`, `@/panels/*`, `@/store/*`, `@/hooks/*`, `@/lib/*`, `@/types/*` all mapping to their respective `src/` subdirectories. Update `vite.config.ts` to add corresponding resolve aliases using the `resolve.alias` option so Vite understands these paths at build time. Install `@types/node` if needed for the path resolution. Test by creating a simple import using an alias to verify resolution works in both IDE and build.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create a test file that imports using a path alias (e.g., `import something from '@/lib/test'`). Run `npm run build` to verify TypeScript resolves the alias correctly. Verify IDE (VS Code) shows no import errors and provides autocomplete for aliased paths.

subtasks.title: Set Up ESLint and Prettier with Project Rules
subtasks.description: Configure ESLint with TypeScript and React hooks plugins, and set up Prettier with the specified formatting rules for consistent code style.
subtasks.details: Install ESLint dependencies: `eslint`, `@typescript-eslint/parser`, `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin`, `eslint-plugin-react`, `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`. Create `.eslintrc.cjs` (or `eslint.config.js` for flat config) with TypeScript parser, React hooks rules enabled, and recommended rulesets. Install Prettier: `prettier`, `eslint-config-prettier`, `eslint-plugin-prettier`. Create `.prettierrc` with: `singleQuote: true`, `semi: false`, `tabWidth: 2`, `useTabs: false`. Add npm scripts: `lint`, `lint:fix`, `format`. Ensure ESLint and Prettier don't conflict by using eslint-config-prettier to disable conflicting rules.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run lint` on the existing scaffold files - should pass with no errors. Intentionally add a formatting violation (e.g., double quotes) and verify `npm run lint` catches it. Run `npm run format` and verify it auto-fixes to single quotes and removes semicolons.

subtasks.title: Create Folder Structure and Install Core Dependencies
subtasks.description: Establish the src/ folder hierarchy for components, panels, store, hooks, lib, and types. Install Zustand, React Query, and create .mira/ directory scaffold.
subtasks.details: Create the following directories under `src/`: `components/` (reusable UI components), `panels/` (panel types: terminal, kanban, companion subdirectories), `store/` (Zustand stores), `hooks/` (custom React hooks), `lib/` (utilities and helpers), `types/` (TypeScript interfaces and types). Add placeholder `index.ts` files in each directory for clean exports. Install core dependencies: `npm install zustand @tanstack/react-query`. Create `.mira/` directory in project root with placeholder YAML files: `config.yml`, `companion.yml`, `skills.yml`, `memory.yml`, and subdirectories `workspaces/`, `themes/`, `journals/`, `snapshots/`. Add `.mira/memory.yml` to `.gitignore` per the two-layer config model.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify all directories exist with `ls -la src/` and `ls -la .mira/`. Verify dependencies installed by checking `package.json` includes zustand and @tanstack/react-query. Import Zustand in a test store file and verify no TypeScript errors. Run `npm run build` to confirm the folder structure doesn't break compilation.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the Vite + React + TypeScript scaffolding into: 1) Initial Vite project creation with React-TS template, 2) TypeScript configuration with strict mode and path aliases, 3) ESLint and Prettier setup with specified rules, 4) Folder structure creation and core dependency installation. Each subtask should be independently verifiable.

tasks.title: Set Up Node.js Local Server with Express/Fastify
tasks.description: Create the Mira Local Server that handles PTY management, WebSocket connections, config file operations, and serves as the backend for the React frontend.
tasks.details:
1. Create `server/` directory at project root
2. Initialize with `package.json` (type: module, TypeScript)
3. Install dependencies: `fastify`, `@fastify/websocket`, `@fastify/cors`, `node-pty`, `ws`, `js-yaml`
4. Create server entry point `server/index.ts`:
   - Fastify instance with CORS configured for localhost
   - WebSocket upgrade handling
   - REST routes placeholder (`/api/config`, `/api/health`)
5. Create modular structure:
   - `server/pty/` - PTY management
   - `server/config/` - .mira/ config read/write
   - `server/ws/` - WebSocket handlers
   - `server/mcp/` - MCP bridge placeholder
6. Configure TypeScript for Node.js ESM
7. Add `tsx` for development, build script for production
8. Bind to 127.0.0.1:3001 (not 0.0.0.0 per security requirements)
tasks.testStrategy: Server starts without errors. `curl http://localhost:3001/api/health` returns 200. WebSocket connection can be established from browser dev tools.

subtasks.title: Initialize Server Directory with Package.json and TypeScript ESM Configuration
subtasks.description: Create the server/ directory at project root and configure it for TypeScript with ES modules support, including all necessary dependencies.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `server/` directory at project root
2. Initialize `package.json` with `type: module` for ESM support
3. Add dependencies: `fastify`, `@fastify/websocket`, `@fastify/cors`, `node-pty`, `ws`, `js-yaml`
4. Add dev dependencies: `typescript`, `tsx`, `@types/node`, `@types/ws`, `@fastify/type-provider-typebox`
5. Create `tsconfig.json` configured for Node.js ESM:
   - `target: ES2022`, `module: NodeNext`, `moduleResolution: NodeNext`
   - `outDir: ./dist`, `rootDir: ./src`
   - `strict: true`, `esModuleInterop: true`
   - `resolveJsonModule: true`
6. Add scripts: `dev` (tsx watch), `build` (tsc), `start` (node dist/index.js)
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm install` successfully. Verify `tsc --noEmit` passes with no errors. Confirm `package.json` has `type: module`.

subtasks.title: Create Fastify Instance with CORS and Health Endpoint
subtasks.description: Set up the main Fastify server entry point with CORS configured for localhost and implement the health check endpoint.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `server/src/index.ts` as the entry point
2. Initialize Fastify instance with logger enabled
3. Register `@fastify/cors` with options:
   - `origin: ['http://localhost:5173', 'http://127.0.0.1:5173']` (Vite dev server)
   - `credentials: true`
4. Implement `/api/health` GET endpoint returning:
   - `{ status: 'ok', timestamp: Date.now(), version: '0.1.0' }`
5. Add `/api/config` route placeholder (returns 501 Not Implemented for now)
6. Bind server to `127.0.0.1:3001` (security requirement - NOT 0.0.0.0)
7. Add graceful shutdown handlers for SIGTERM and SIGINT
8. Export server instance for testing
subtasks.testStrategy: Start server with `npm run dev`. Verify `curl http://localhost:3001/api/health` returns 200 with JSON body. Confirm server only accepts connections from localhost (not accessible from other machines).

subtasks.title: Implement WebSocket Upgrade Handling
subtasks.description: Set up WebSocket support using @fastify/websocket for real-time terminal I/O and event streaming between frontend and server.
subtasks.details:
1. Register `@fastify/websocket` plugin on Fastify instance
2. Create WebSocket route at `/ws` with connection handler:
   - Accept connection upgrade
   - Log connection/disconnection events
   - Implement basic ping/pong keepalive
3. Define message type enum: `pty:output`, `pty:input`, `pty:resize`, `pty:status`, `config:update`, `event:notification`
4. Create typed WebSocket message interface in `server/src/types/ws-protocol.ts`
5. Implement message parsing and routing stub (dispatches to appropriate handler based on type)
6. Add error handling for malformed messages
7. Track active connections with Map<connectionId, WebSocket>
subtasks.testStrategy: Use browser dev tools or wscat to establish WebSocket connection to ws://localhost:3001/ws. Verify connection succeeds. Send a JSON message and confirm server logs receipt. Test ping/pong keepalive works.

subtasks.title: Create Modular Directory Structure for PTY/Config/WS/MCP
subtasks.description: Establish the modular server architecture with dedicated directories and placeholder modules for PTY management, config operations, WebSocket handlers, and MCP bridge.
subtasks.details:
1. Create directory structure:
   - `server/src/pty/` - PTY management module
   - `server/src/config/` - .mira/ config read/write operations
   - `server/src/ws/` - WebSocket message handlers
   - `server/src/mcp/` - MCP bridge placeholder
   - `server/src/types/` - Shared TypeScript types
2. Create placeholder modules with exported interfaces:
   - `pty/index.ts` - PtyManager class stub (create, resize, write, kill session methods)
   - `config/index.ts` - ConfigEngine class stub (read, write, watch .mira/ methods)
   - `ws/index.ts` - WebSocketHandler class stub (onMessage, broadcast methods)
   - `mcp/index.ts` - McpBridge class stub (connect, disconnect, listTools methods)
3. Add barrel exports (`index.ts`) for each module
4. Define core interfaces: `PtySession`, `ConfigFile`, `WsConnection`, `McpServer`
subtasks.testStrategy: TypeScript compilation succeeds with no errors. All modules can be imported from their barrel exports. Interfaces are properly typed and exported.

subtasks.title: Configure Development and Build Scripts with tsx
subtasks.description: Set up the development workflow using tsx for hot-reload development and configure production build scripts.
subtasks.details:
1. Add `tsx` as dev dependency for TypeScript execution without compilation step
2. Configure package.json scripts:
   - `dev`: `tsx watch src/index.ts` - Hot-reload development server
   - `build`: `tsc` - Compile TypeScript to JavaScript
   - `start`: `node dist/index.js` - Run production build
   - `typecheck`: `tsc --noEmit` - Type checking only
3. Add `.env` support with `dotenv` for environment variables:
   - `PORT=3001` (default)
   - `HOST=127.0.0.1` (security: localhost only)
   - `LOG_LEVEL=info`
4. Create `server/.env.example` with documented variables
5. Add `nodemon.json` or tsx config for watching specific directories
6. Ensure dist/ is in .gitignore
7. Add npm script `clean`: `rm -rf dist`
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run dev` - server starts with hot reload. Make a code change - server restarts automatically. Run `npm run build` - dist/ folder created with compiled JS. Run `npm start` - production server starts from dist/.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the server setup into: 1) Server directory initialization with package.json and TypeScript config for ESM, 2) Fastify instance with CORS and health endpoint, 3) WebSocket upgrade handling setup, 4) Modular directory structure for pty/config/ws/mcp, 5) Development and build scripts with tsx. Include security binding to 127.0.0.1.

tasks.title: Implement Config Engine for .mira/ Directory
tasks.description: Build the Config Engine that reads, writes, and watches .mira/*.yml files, syncing them with frontend state via Zustand. Implements the two-layer config model (portable vs personal memory).
tasks.details:
1. Create `server/config/ConfigEngine.ts`:
   - `loadConfig(projectPath)` - reads all .mira/*.yml files
   - `saveConfig(key, data)` - writes to specific YAML file with <100ms target
   - `watchConfig()` - file watcher using chokidar for external changes
2. Define TypeScript interfaces in `server/types/config.ts`:
   - `MiraConfig` (config.yml schema)
   - `CompanionConfig` (companion.yml)
   - `WorkspaceConfig` (workspaces/*.yml)
   - `SkillsConfig` (skills.yml)
3. Implement YAML serialization with `js-yaml`:
   - Custom serializers for complex types
   - Schema validation on load
4. Create debounced auto-save (300ms debounce)
5. Implement `.mira/` directory structure initialization:
   ```
   .mira/
     config.yml
     companion.yml
     workspaces/default.yml
     skills.yml
     themes/
     journals/
     snapshots/
   ```
6. Add REST endpoints: `GET/PUT /api/config/:file`
7. Handle personal memory layer (memory.yml) separately with gitignore awareness
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with Vitest: write config → read config roundtrip. Integration test: modify YAML externally → change detected within 500ms. Verify <100ms write time.

subtasks.title: Define TypeScript Interfaces for Config Schemas
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive TypeScript interfaces for all .mira/ config file schemas including MiraConfig, CompanionConfig, WorkspaceConfig, SkillsConfig, and supporting types.
subtasks.details:
Create `server/types/config.ts` with:
- `MiraConfig` interface for config.yml (core settings, enabled modules, version)
- `CompanionConfig` interface for companion.yml (personality settings, memory preferences, LLM provider config)
- `WorkspaceConfig` interface for workspaces/*.yml (panel layout, toggle states, active scene)
- `SkillsConfig` interface for skills.yml (installed skills array with name, version, enabled flag, permissions)
- `ThemeConfig` interface for themes/*.yml
- `MemoryConfig` interface for memory.yml (personal layer, session history, observations)
- `SIConfig` interface for project_SI.yml (hypotheses, cycle history)
- Union type `ConfigLayer = 'portable' | 'personal'` for two-layer model
- Helper type `ConfigFileMap` mapping file paths to their config types
- Validation schemas using zod or similar for runtime type checking
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with Vitest: validate that sample YAML data parses correctly into interfaces. Test type guards for each config type. Verify zod schemas reject malformed data with descriptive errors.

subtasks.title: Implement ConfigEngine Class with Load/Save Methods
subtasks.description: Build the core ConfigEngine class with loadConfig and saveConfig methods using js-yaml, implementing the two-layer config model with portable vs personal memory separation.
subtasks.details:
Create `server/config/ConfigEngine.ts`:
- Constructor accepts `projectPath` and initializes internal state
- `loadConfig(projectPath)`: reads all .mira/*.yml files, parses with js-yaml, validates against schemas, returns typed config object
- `saveConfig(key: ConfigKey, data: ConfigType)`: serializes to YAML, writes to appropriate file, target <100ms write time
- `getConfigPath(key)`: resolves config key to file path
- `isPersonalLayer(key)`: determines if config belongs to gitignored personal layer
- Custom js-yaml serializers for Date objects, Map/Set types, and nested configs
- Implement caching layer to avoid redundant disk reads
- `loadAll()`: batch load all config files on startup
- `getConfig<T>(key)`: typed getter with generics
- Error handling with custom `ConfigError` class for parse/write failures
- Performance: use fs.promises for async I/O, benchmark write operations
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: write config → read config roundtrip preserves data. Test custom serializers with complex types. Benchmark saveConfig to verify <100ms write time. Test error handling for invalid YAML, missing files, permission errors.

subtasks.title: Implement File Watcher with Chokidar
subtasks.description: Create a file watcher implementation using chokidar that detects external changes to .mira/*.yml files and emits events for syncing with frontend state.
subtasks.details:
Add to `server/config/ConfigEngine.ts` or create `server/config/ConfigWatcher.ts`:
- `watchConfig()`: initializes chokidar watcher on .mira/ directory
- Watch patterns: `**/*.yml` excluding node_modules and .git
- Event handlers for 'add', 'change', 'unlink' events
- `onConfigChange(callback)`: register listeners for config changes
- Debounce rapid changes (100ms) to avoid duplicate events
- Emit typed events: `{ file: string, type: 'added'|'changed'|'removed', config: ConfigType }`
- `unwatchConfig()`: cleanup watcher on shutdown
- Handle atomic writes (temp file → rename pattern)
- Ignore changes from internal saves (track in-flight writes)
- EventEmitter pattern for broadcasting changes to multiple subscribers
- Graceful handling of watcher errors (permission denied, directory removed)
- Integration point for WebSocket broadcast to frontend
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: modify YAML file externally → verify change event fires within 500ms. Test debouncing: rapid changes produce single event. Test ignore self-writes: internal save doesn't trigger change event. Test watcher cleanup on shutdown.

subtasks.title: Implement Debounced Auto-Save Mechanism
subtasks.description: Create a debounced auto-save system with 300ms debounce that batches rapid config changes and writes them efficiently to disk.
subtasks.details:
Create `server/config/AutoSave.ts` or integrate into ConfigEngine:
- `queueSave(key: ConfigKey, data: ConfigType)`: adds save to pending queue
- Debounce implementation with 300ms delay using lodash.debounce or custom
- Batch multiple pending saves into single disk operation cycle
- `flushPendingWrites()`: force immediate write of all pending changes
- Track dirty state per config file
- `getPendingChanges()`: introspection for debugging
- Handle save failures: retry logic with exponential backoff (max 3 retries)
- Emit 'saved' event after successful write with timing metrics
- `cancelPendingWrites()`: abort pending saves on shutdown
- Ensure write order preserved for dependent configs
- Memory pressure handling: flush if pending queue exceeds threshold
- Integration with ConfigEngine.saveConfig for actual disk I/O
- Logging for save operations with duration metrics
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: queue 10 rapid changes → only 1-2 disk writes occur. Test debounce timing: changes within 300ms batch together. Test flush: flushPendingWrites immediately persists. Test failure recovery: simulated write failure triggers retry.

subtasks.title: Implement .mira/ Directory Structure Initialization
subtasks.description: Build the directory scaffold system that initializes the complete .mira/ directory structure with default config files for new projects.
subtasks.details:
Create `server/config/ConfigInitializer.ts`:
- `initializeMiraDirectory(projectPath)`: creates full .mira/ structure
- Directory structure:
  ```
  .mira/
    config.yml           # Core settings with sensible defaults
    companion.yml        # Default Mira personality
    workspaces/default.yml  # Default workspace layout
    skills.yml           # Empty skills list
    themes/              # Empty, ready for custom themes
    journals/            # Empty, auto-populated during use
    snapshots/           # Empty, for workspace snapshots
    memory.yml           # Personal layer (gitignored)
    project_SI.yml       # SI tracking (gitignored by default)
  ```
- `isInitialized(projectPath)`: check if .mira/ exists and is valid
- `getDefaultConfig(type)`: returns default config for each file type
- Generate .gitignore entries for personal layer files (memory.yml, user_SI.yml)
- Atomic initialization: create temp dir → populate → rename
- `migrateConfig(fromVersion, toVersion)`: handle config schema upgrades
- Idempotent: safe to call on existing .mira/ directories
- Preserve existing configs during re-initialization
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: initialize on empty dir → all files created with valid defaults. Test idempotent: initialize twice → no data loss. Verify .gitignore contains memory.yml. Test migration: old config format → upgraded correctly.

subtasks.title: Create REST Endpoints for Config CRUD
subtasks.description: Implement REST API endpoints for config file operations with proper handling of the two-layer model (portable vs personal) and validation.
subtasks.details:
Create `server/routes/config.ts` with Express/Fastify routes:
- `GET /api/config/:file`: read specific config file, returns parsed JSON
- `PUT /api/config/:file`: update config file, accepts JSON body, triggers debounced save
- `GET /api/config`: list all available config files with metadata (layer, lastModified)
- `POST /api/config/init`: initialize .mira/ directory for project
- `GET /api/config/status`: health check with pending writes count, watcher status
- Request validation middleware using zod schemas from subtask 1
- Response format: `{ data: ConfigType, meta: { layer: 'portable'|'personal', file: string } }`
- Error responses: 400 for validation errors, 404 for missing files, 500 for I/O errors
- Layer awareness: personal layer files (memory.yml) have restricted access patterns
- WebSocket endpoint `/ws/config` for real-time config change notifications
- Rate limiting: max 100 writes/minute per client
- Authentication hook for future security (currently no-op)
- OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for all endpoints
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: GET returns valid config, PUT updates file on disk. Test validation: malformed JSON rejected with 400. Test WebSocket: config change broadcasts to connected clients. Test rate limiting: exceed limit → 429 response.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the Config Engine into: 1) TypeScript interfaces for all config schemas (MiraConfig, CompanionConfig, WorkspaceConfig, SkillsConfig), 2) ConfigEngine class with loadConfig and saveConfig methods using js-yaml, 3) File watcher implementation with chokidar for external changes, 4) Debounced auto-save mechanism (300ms), 5) Directory structure initialization for .mira/ scaffold, 6) REST endpoints for config CRUD with two-layer model (portable vs personal). Target <100ms write time.

tasks.title: Build Panel Layout Engine with Drag-and-Drop
tasks.description: Implement the modular drag-and-drop panel layout system using react-grid-layout that allows users to arrange, resize, and persist panel configurations to .mira/workspaces/*.yml.
tasks.details:
1. Install `react-grid-layout` (or evaluate `allotment` for split panes)
2. Create `src/components/Layout/PanelGrid.tsx`:
   - Responsive grid with breakpoints
   - Minimum panel sizes (240x180px)
   - Panel snap-to-grid behavior
3. Create `src/components/Layout/Panel.tsx`:
   - Generic panel wrapper with header (title, close, maximize)
   - Panel content slot
   - Drag handle
4. Create `src/store/layoutStore.ts` (Zustand):
   - `panels: PanelConfig[]` - position, size, type, id
   - `addPanel()`, `removePanel()`, `updateLayout()`
   - Persist to .mira/workspaces/[name].yml via config engine
5. Implement layout serialization:
   ```typescript
   interface PanelConfig {
     id: string;
     type: 'terminal' | 'kanban' | 'companion' | 'mcp-status';
     position: { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number };
     props?: Record<string, unknown>;
   }
   ```
6. Target <16ms frame time during drag operations
7. Handle panel z-index for overlapping
tasks.testStrategy: E2E test with Playwright: drag panel → verify position updates in store → verify persisted to YAML. Performance test: measure frame time during drag (should be <16ms).

subtasks.title: Install and Configure react-grid-layout with Responsive Breakpoints
subtasks.description: Install react-grid-layout package and create the foundational PanelGrid component with responsive breakpoints for different screen sizes.
subtasks.details: Install `react-grid-layout` and `@types/react-grid-layout`. Create `src/components/Layout/PanelGrid.tsx` with ResponsiveGridLayout. Configure breakpoints: lg (1200px), md (996px), sm (768px), xs (480px). Set column counts per breakpoint (lg: 12, md: 10, sm: 6, xs: 4). Configure rowHeight based on viewport. Enable dragging and resizing with `isDraggable` and `isResizable` props. Set `compactType` to null for free-form positioning. Add CSS imports for react-grid-layout styles.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify grid renders at each breakpoint with correct column count. Test that layout recalculates on window resize. Confirm drag and resize are enabled by default.

subtasks.title: Define PanelConfig TypeScript Interface and Type System
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive TypeScript interfaces for panel configuration including position, size, type, and extensible props.
subtasks.details: Create `src/types/layout.ts` with interfaces: `PanelConfig { id: string; type: PanelType; position: PanelPosition; props?: Record<string, unknown>; minimized?: boolean; maximized?: boolean; }`. Define `PanelPosition { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number; minW: number; minH: number; }`. Create `PanelType` union: 'terminal' | 'kanban' | 'companion' | 'mcp-status' | 'journal' | 'custom'. Add `WorkspaceLayout { name: string; panels: PanelConfig[]; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string; }`. Set minimum size constants: MIN_WIDTH = 2 (grid units ≈ 240px), MIN_HEIGHT = 2 (grid units ≈ 180px).
subtasks.testStrategy: TypeScript compilation succeeds. Type guards validate PanelConfig objects correctly. Test that invalid panel types are rejected at compile time.

subtasks.title: Create Generic Panel Wrapper Component with Header and Controls
subtasks.description: Build the reusable Panel component with header bar containing title, drag handle, and window controls (minimize, maximize, close).
subtasks.details: Create `src/components/Layout/Panel.tsx` as a generic wrapper. Header section: drag handle (className='drag-handle'), title text, control buttons (minimize, maximize, close). Use React.forwardRef for grid-layout compatibility. Props: `id`, `title`, `type`, `children`, `onClose`, `onMaximize`, `onMinimize`, `isMaximized`. Content area with overflow handling. CSS modules or Tailwind for styling. Drag handle must have `cursor: grab/grabbing`. Close button calls parent handler. Maximize toggles between normal and full-grid size. Apply minimum size constraints visually (prevent shrinking below 240x180px).
subtasks.testStrategy: Panel renders with all header controls visible. Drag handle initiates drag when grabbed. Close/maximize/minimize buttons trigger respective callbacks. Content slot renders children correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement Zustand Layout Store with Panel CRUD Operations
subtasks.description: Create the Zustand store for managing panel state including add, remove, update, and layout change operations with proper TypeScript typing.
subtasks.details: Create `src/store/layoutStore.ts` using Zustand. State shape: `{ panels: PanelConfig[]; activeWorkspace: string; isDirty: boolean; }`. Actions: `addPanel(config: Omit<PanelConfig, 'id'>)` - generates UUID, `removePanel(id: string)`, `updatePanel(id: string, updates: Partial<PanelConfig>)`, `updateLayout(layouts: Layout[])` - syncs react-grid-layout changes, `setActiveWorkspace(name: string)`, `resetLayout()`. Use immer middleware for immutable updates. Add selector hooks: `usePanel(id)`, `usePanelsByType(type)`. Track dirty state for unsaved changes. Generate unique IDs with `crypto.randomUUID()` or nanoid.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: addPanel creates unique ID and adds to array. removePanel filters correctly. updateLayout syncs positions. Dirty flag sets on changes and clears on save.

subtasks.title: Build Layout Serialization to .mira/workspaces/*.yml via Config Engine
subtasks.description: Implement bidirectional serialization between Zustand layout state and YAML workspace files using the Config Engine from Task 3.
subtasks.details: Create `src/services/layoutSerializer.ts`. Export functions: `serializeLayout(panels: PanelConfig[]): WorkspaceYAML` - converts to YAML-friendly structure, `deserializeLayout(yaml: WorkspaceYAML): PanelConfig[]` - parses YAML to typed configs. tasks.title: Implement PTY Manager with node-pty
tasks.description: Create the PTY Manager service that spawns and manages pseudo-terminal processes for agent terminals. Supports multiple concurrent PTY sessions with session persistence.
tasks.details:
1. Create `server/pty/PtyManager.ts`:
   - `spawnSession(id, shell, args, cwd)` - create new PTY
   - `writeToSession(id, data)` - send input to PTY
   - `resizeSession(id, cols, rows)` - handle terminal resize
   - `killSession(id)` - terminate PTY
   - `getSession(id)` - retrieve session info
2. Session tracking with Map<string, PtySession>:
   ```typescript
   interface PtySession {
     id: string;
     pty: IPty;
     status: 'idle' | 'thinking' | 'running' | 'error';
     createdAt: Date;
     outputBuffer: string[]; // Last N lines for reconnect
   }
   ```
3. Output buffer: retain last 5000 lines per session for history
4. Implement status detection heuristics:
   - 'running': recent stdout activity
   - 'idle': no activity for 2s
   - 'error': process exit code !== 0
5. Handle graceful shutdown: SIGTERM → SIGKILL after 5s
6. Target: 8+ concurrent terminals per PRD scalability requirements
7. Create WebSocket message protocol for PTY I/O
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test: spawn bash session, write 'echo hello', verify 'hello' in output. Integration test: 8 concurrent sessions without degradation. Test session persistence: disconnect/reconnect retains output buffer.

subtasks.title: Create PtyManager Class with Core Session Methods
subtasks.description: Implement the foundational PtyManager class in server/pty/PtyManager.ts with methods for spawning, writing to, resizing, killing, and retrieving PTY sessions using node-pty.
subtasks.details:
Create `server/pty/PtyManager.ts` as a singleton class that wraps node-pty functionality. Implement core methods:
- `spawnSession(id: string, shell: string, args: string[], cwd: string): PtySession` - spawns a new PTY process with node-pty's `spawn()`, configures initial cols/rows (default 80x24), sets up data event listeners
- `writeToSession(id: string, data: string): void` - sends input data to the PTY via `pty.write()`
- `resizeSession(id: string, cols: number, rows: number): void` - handles terminal resize via `pty.resize()`
- `killSession(id: string): void` - terminates the PTY process (basic implementation, graceful shutdown in subtask 5)
- `getSession(id: string): PtySession | undefined` - retrieves session from internal Map

Define the PtySession interface with id, pty (IPty), status, createdAt, and outputBuffer fields. Initialize the sessions Map<string, PtySession> in constructor. Add proper TypeScript types from @types/node-pty.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: spawn a bash session, verify PtySession object returned with correct id and 'running' status. Test writeToSession with 'echo hello' command. Test getSession returns the correct session. Test killSession removes session from Map.

subtasks.title: Implement Session Tracking with Status Management
subtasks.description: Build the session tracking system using Map<string, PtySession> with proper status management for 'idle', 'thinking', 'running', and 'error' states.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: spawn multiple sessions, verify all tracked in Map. Test session enumeration returns correct count. Test status transitions are tracked correctly. Test concurrent session creation doesn't cause race conditions.

subtasks.title: Implement Output Buffer Management
subtasks.description: Create the output buffer system that retains the last 5000 lines per session for history and reconnection support.
subtasks.details:
Implement a circular buffer for each PTY session's output:

1. Create `server/pty/OutputBuffer.ts` class:
   - `private buffer: string[]` with configurable max size (default 5000)
   - `push(line: string): void` - adds line, removes oldest if at capacity
   - `getLines(start?: number, end?: number): string[]` - retrieve lines slice
   - `getAll(): string[]` - get entire buffer content
   - `clear(): void` - empty the buffer
   - `getLineCount(): number` - current buffer size

2. Integrate with PtyManager:
   - Attach OutputBuffer instance to each PtySession
   - Hook into PTY 'data' event, parse into lines, push to buffer
   - Handle partial lines (data chunks may not end at newline)
   - Implement `getSessionOutput(id: string, lastN?: number): string[]`

3. Memory management:
   - Each buffer max ~5000 lines × ~200 chars = ~1MB per session
   - For 8 sessions target = ~8MB total, acceptable memory footprint
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: push 6000 lines, verify only last 5000 retained. Test getLines with various ranges. Test partial line handling across multiple data events. Memory test: verify buffer doesn't exceed expected size with large outputs.

subtasks.title: Implement Status Detection Heuristics
subtasks.description: Build the automatic status detection system that determines session state (idle, thinking, running, error) based on activity patterns and process exit codes.
subtasks.details:
Create intelligent status detection in `server/pty/StatusDetector.ts`:

1. Activity-based detection:
   - 'running': stdout activity within last 2 seconds
   - 'idle': no stdout/stderr activity for 2+ seconds
   - 'thinking': pattern-based detection (Claude output patterns like 'Thinking...', spinner chars)
   - 'error': process exit code !== 0

2. Implementation:
   - Create `StatusDetector` class that monitors PTY sessions
   - Use `setInterval` (every 500ms) to check activity timestamps
   - Pattern matching for 'thinking' state: detect AI agent progress indicators
   - Hook into PTY 'exit' event for error detection
   - Debounce rapid status changes to avoid flickering

3. Integration with PtyManager:
   - `private statusDetector: StatusDetector` instance
   - Auto-start detection when session spawned
   - Stop detection when session killed
   - Emit 'status-change' events for UI updates

4. Configurable thresholds:
   - `idleTimeout: number` (default 2000ms)
   - `thinkingPatterns: RegExp[]` (configurable patterns)
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: simulate stdout activity, verify 'running' status. Wait 3 seconds, verify 'idle' status. Test exit code 1 triggers 'error' status. Test 'thinking' pattern detection with mock AI output. Test status debouncing prevents rapid changes.

subtasks.title: Implement Graceful Shutdown Handling
subtasks.description: Create the graceful shutdown system that sends SIGTERM first, then SIGKILL after 5 seconds if the process doesn't terminate.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: spawn long-running process, killSession(), verify SIGTERM sent first. Test timeout: mock process that ignores SIGTERM, verify SIGKILL sent after 5s. Test force kill bypasses SIGTERM. Integration test: server shutdown terminates all PTY sessions cleanly.

subtasks.title: Create WebSocket Message Protocol for PTY I/O
subtasks.description: Define and implement the WebSocket message protocol for bidirectional PTY communication between the server and browser terminals.
subtasks.details:
Create `server/pty/PtyWebSocket.ts` with message protocol:

1. Message types (client → server):
   ```typescript
   type ClientMessage = 
     | { type: 'spawn'; id: string; shell: string; args: string[]; cwd: string }
     | { type: 'input'; id: string; data: string }
     | { type: 'resize'; id: string; cols: number; rows: number }
     | { type: 'kill'; id: string }
     | { type: 'reconnect'; id: string }  // Request buffer replay
   ```

2. Message types (server → client):
   ```typescript
   type ServerMessage =
     | { type: 'output'; id: string; data: string }
     | { type: 'status'; id: string; status: SessionStatus }
     | { type: 'exit'; id: string; code: number }
     | { type: 'error'; id: string; message: string }
     | { type: 'buffer'; id: string; lines: string[] }  // Reconnect replay
   ```

3. WebSocket handler integration:
   - Create `PtyWebSocketHandler` class
   - Parse incoming messages, route to PtyManager methods
   - Subscribe to PTY output events, broadcast to connected clients
   - Handle client disconnection (keep PTY alive for reconnect)
   - Implement heartbeat/ping-pong for connection health

4. Multiplexing:
   - Single WebSocket connection handles multiple PTY sessions
   - Session ID in every message for routing
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: WebSocket client sends 'spawn' message, verify session created. Test 'input' message flows to PTY. Test 'output' messages received on client. Test 'reconnect' replays buffer. Load test: 8 concurrent sessions over single WebSocket, verify no message loss or corruption.

Target 8+ concurrent terminals.

tasks.title: Create WebSocket Communication Layer

tasks.details:

   ```typescript
   type WsMessage = 
     | { type: 'pty:output'; sessionId: string; data: string }
     | { type: 'pty:input'; sessionId: string; data: string }
     | { type: 'pty:resize'; sessionId: string; cols: number; rows: number }
     | { type: 'pty:status'; sessionId: string; status: PtyStatus }
     | { type: 'config:update'; file: string; data: object }
     | { type: 'event:notification'; payload: Notification };
   ```
2. Server: `server/ws/WsHandler.ts`:
   - Connection management with heartbeat (30s ping/pong)
   - Route messages to appropriate service (PTY, Config)
   - Binary support for terminal data (Uint8Array)
3. Client: `src/lib/ws/WsClient.ts`:
   - Singleton WebSocket instance
   - Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s)
   - Message queue during disconnect
4. Create React hook `src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts`:
   - Subscribe to message types
   - Send messages with type safety
5. Implement multiplexing: single WS connection for all terminals
6. Measure and log latency: target <50ms for terminal input→output
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: send pty:input, measure time to pty:output callback (<50ms). Test reconnection: kill server → restart → verify auto-reconnect and message queue flush.

subtasks.title: Define Shared WebSocket Message Protocol Types

Include PtyStatus enum (connecting, connected, disconnected, error). Add helper type guards like isPtyMessage() and isConfigMessage(). Export message factory functions for type-safe message creation. Include timestamp field on all messages for latency measurement.

subtasks.title: Implement Server-Side WebSocket Handler with Connection Management

subtasks.details: Create WsHandler class using 'ws' library. Implement connection pool tracking clients by unique connectionId. Set up 30-second ping/pong heartbeat cycle - send ping every 30s, terminate connections that miss 2 consecutive pongs. Route incoming messages to appropriate service handlers (PtyManager for pty:* messages, ConfigEngine for config:* messages). Support binary WebSocket frames for terminal data using Uint8Array. Add message validation against protocol types before routing. Implement broadcast capability for sending to specific sessionId subscribers. Log connection events and errors. Add latency tracking by echoing timestamps on pty:output messages.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: verify heartbeat terminates stale connections, messages route to correct handlers, binary frames decode properly, malformed messages are rejected gracefully.

subtasks.title: Build Client-Side WebSocket Singleton with Auto-Reconnect

subtasks.details: Implement WsClient as singleton class with private constructor. Manage WebSocket connection lifecycle (connecting, connected, disconnecting, disconnected states). Implement exponential backoff reconnection: delays of 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, capped at 30s max. Reset backoff on successful connection. Queue outgoing messages when disconnected (max 100 messages, drop oldest). Flush queue in order upon reconnection. Support binary data sending for terminal input. Emit connection state events for UI feedback. Add send() method that returns Promise resolving when message acknowledged or rejects on timeout. Track round-trip latency by measuring pty:input to pty:output time. Provide connect(), disconnect(), and isConnected() methods.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: verify exponential backoff timing, message queue limits enforced, queue flushes on reconnect. Integration test: simulate disconnect → queue messages → reconnect → verify delivery order.

Provide subscribe<T>(messageType, callback) for type-safe message handling - callback receives only messages matching the type. Return unsubscribe function for cleanup in useEffect. Provide send<T>(message: T) method with TypeScript generic constraint to WsMessage union. Expose connectionState reactive value (connecting, connected, disconnected, reconnecting). Expose latency metric as reactive value updated on each pty round-trip. Add useTerminalMessages(sessionId) specialized hook that filters to specific terminal session. Add useConfigUpdates() hook for config:update subscriptions. Ensure proper cleanup on component unmount to prevent memory leaks.

Implement client-side session management in WsClient - track subscribed sessionIds, filter incoming messages by sessionId. Add LatencyMonitor utility class tracking: per-message round-trip time (inject timestamp in pty:input, measure delta on pty:output), rolling average over last 100 messages, p50/p95/p99 percentiles. Log warnings when latency exceeds 50ms threshold. Expose latency metrics via useWebSocket hook. Add performance marks using Performance API for debugging. Implement message prioritization - pty:input/output get priority over config:update. Test with 4+ concurrent terminal sessions to verify multiplexing doesn't degrade latency.

tasks.title: Build Zustand State Management with Config Sync
tasks.description: Set up comprehensive Zustand state management that syncs with .mira/ config files. Implement middleware for persistence, devtools, and config serialization.
tasks.details:
1. Create core stores in `src/store/`:
   - `layoutStore.ts` - panel positions, sizes
   - `toggleStore.ts` - module visibility
   - `sessionStore.ts` - agent sessions, active workspace
   - `companionStore.ts` - chat history, context
   - `configStore.ts` - mirrors .mira/ files
2. Create Zustand middleware `src/store/middleware/configSync.ts`:
   - Debounced persist to server (300ms)
   - Optimistic updates (immediate UI, async persist)
   - Conflict resolution (server wins with notification)
3. Implement store hydration from config on app load:
   ```typescript
   const useConfigStore = create<ConfigState>()(persist(
     (set, get) => ({ /* state */ }),
     { name: 'mira-config', storage: createConfigStorage() }
   ));
   ```
4. Create `createConfigStorage()` adapter:
   - getItem → fetch from server `/api/config/:file`
   - setItem → PUT to server with debounce
5. Add devtools integration for debugging
6. Type-safe selectors with `useShallow` for performance
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test: modify store → verify server receives PUT request. Test optimistic updates: verify UI updates before server response. Integration test: server config change → store updates via WebSocket.

subtasks.title: Create Core Store Definitions with TypeScript Interfaces
subtasks.description: Define all five core Zustand stores (layoutStore, toggleStore, sessionStore, companionStore, configStore) with comprehensive TypeScript interfaces and initial state structure.
subtasks.details: Create `src/store/` directory with individual store files. Define TypeScript interfaces for each store's state shape: LayoutState (panel positions, sizes, z-index), ToggleState (module visibility flags, collapsed states), SessionState (agent sessions map, active workspace ID, connection status), CompanionState (chat history array, current context, streaming state), ConfigState (mirrors .mira/ file structure - config.yml, workspaces/, skills.yml). Each store should export its interface, initial state, and basic actions (set, reset, update). Use Zustand's StateCreator pattern for composability. Ensure all stores follow consistent naming conventions and export patterns for later middleware integration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test each store: verify initial state shape matches interface, test basic actions (set/reset) update state correctly, verify TypeScript compilation catches type mismatches.

subtasks.title: Implement configSync Middleware with Debounced Persistence
subtasks.description: Create Zustand middleware that intercepts state changes and persists them to the server via REST API with 300ms debounce to prevent excessive network requests.
subtasks.details: Create `src/store/middleware/configSync.ts` implementing Zustand middleware pattern. Use lodash debounce (or custom implementation) with 300ms delay. Middleware intercepts `set` calls, allows immediate state update, then queues debounced PUT request to `/api/config/:file`. Track pending sync status per config file. Handle batch updates - if multiple state changes occur within debounce window, combine into single request. Implement sync queue to ensure ordering. Add `isSyncing` and `lastSyncedAt` metadata to track persistence state. Export middleware factory that accepts config (debounce time, API base URL). Handle cases where component unmounts during pending debounce - flush immediately or cancel based on configuration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: trigger multiple state changes rapidly, verify only one PUT request sent after 300ms. Test debounce reset: change state, wait 200ms, change again, verify single request 300ms after last change. Mock timer tests for precise debounce verification.

subtasks.title: Build Optimistic Update Pattern with Conflict Resolution
subtasks.description: Implement optimistic updates that apply state changes immediately to UI while handling server conflicts with 'server wins' strategy and user notification system.
subtasks.details: Extend configSync middleware with optimistic update tracking. Maintain `pendingUpdates` map storing original state before each optimistic change. On successful server response, clear pending entry. On conflict (409 status or version mismatch), revert to server state and trigger notification. Create `src/store/middleware/conflictResolver.ts` with strategies: 'server-wins' (default), 'client-wins', 'merge'. Implement notification system via dedicated `notificationStore` or callback pattern - notify user 'Config was updated externally, your changes were reverted'. Track config versions using ETags or custom version field. Handle network failures: retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts), then revert with error notification. Ensure UI never shows stale state - always reflect either optimistic local or confirmed server state.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test optimistic flow: change state → verify immediate UI update → mock server success → verify no revert. Test conflict: change state → mock 409 response → verify state reverts to server value and notification triggered. Test network failure with retry exhaustion.

subtasks.title: Implement Store Hydration with createConfigStorage Adapter
subtasks.description: Create custom Zustand storage adapter that hydrates stores from server config on app load and handles the bidirectional sync between Zustand persist and .mira/ config files.
subtasks.details: Create `src/store/adapters/configStorage.ts` implementing Zustand's `PersistStorage` interface. `getItem(name)`: fetch from `/api/config/:file`, parse YAML response, transform to store shape, handle 404 (return null for new configs). `setItem(name, value)`: serialize to YAML, PUT to server (delegate to configSync middleware for debouncing). `removeItem(name)`: DELETE from server. Create `createConfigStorage(configFile: string)` factory returning configured adapter. Implement initial hydration flow: app loads → fetch all config files in parallel → hydrate stores → mark `isHydrated: true`. Handle partial hydration failures gracefully - use defaults for missing configs. Add loading states to prevent UI flicker during hydration. Support both JSON and YAML serialization based on config file extension.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: mock server with config data → initialize stores → verify hydrated state matches server. Test 404 handling: verify default state used. Test hydration timing: verify `isHydrated` flag transitions correctly and UI waits for hydration.

subtasks.title: Create Type-Safe Selectors with useShallow Performance Optimization
subtasks.description: Implement type-safe selector patterns using useShallow to prevent unnecessary re-renders and create reusable selector utilities across all stores.
subtasks.details: Create `src/store/selectors/` directory with selector files per store. Use Zustand's `useShallow` from 'zustand/react/shallow' for object/array selections. Create selector factory utilities: `createSelector<T, R>(store, selector)` with memoization. Define common selector patterns: `useLayoutPanels()`, `useToggleState(moduleId)`, `useActiveSession()`, `useCompanionMessages()`, `useConfigValue(path)`. Implement derived selectors combining multiple stores safely. Add TypeScript generics ensuring selector return types match store state. Create `src/store/hooks.ts` exporting all typed hooks. Document selector usage patterns with JSDoc. Implement selector composition for complex derived state (e.g., `useActiveAgentWithLayout()` combining session + layout stores). Add performance markers for debugging selector frequency.
subtasks.testStrategy: Performance test: render component with selector → update unrelated state → verify no re-render via React DevTools or render counter. Unit test: verify selector return types match expected interface. Test useShallow: update array item → verify shallow comparison prevents re-render when reference unchanged.

subtasks.title: Integrate Redux DevTools for Store Debugging
subtasks.description: Configure Zustand devtools middleware for all stores enabling time-travel debugging, action logging, and state inspection in Redux DevTools browser extension.
subtasks.details: Import `devtools` from 'zustand/middleware' and wrap all store creators. Configure devtools options: `name` (store identifier in DevTools), `enabled` (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'), `anonymousActionType` for unnamed actions. Create store composition pattern: `devtools(persist(configSync(storeImpl)))` with correct middleware ordering - devtools outermost for full visibility. Add action names to all state updates using Zustand's `set` third parameter: `set(newState, false, 'actionName')`. Implement custom action serialization for complex objects (PTY sessions, WebSocket connections). Create `src/store/devtools.ts` with helper to wrap stores consistently. Document middleware ordering requirements. Add trace option for action stack traces in development. Configure devtools to handle large state objects (panel layouts, chat history) without performance degradation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual test: install Redux DevTools extension → perform state changes → verify actions appear with correct names and payloads. Test time-travel: revert to previous state → verify UI reflects historical state. Verify devtools disabled in production build.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the Zustand state management into: 1) Core store definitions (layoutStore, toggleStore, sessionStore, companionStore, configStore), 2) configSync middleware with debounced persistence (300ms) to server, 3) Optimistic update pattern with conflict resolution (server wins + notification), 4) Store hydration from config on app load with createConfigStorage adapter, 5) Type-safe selectors with useShallow for performance, 6) Devtools integration for debugging. Handle server round-trip for persistence.

subtasks.title: Implement quickPromptStore with State Management
subtasks.description: Create the Zustand store for Quick-Prompt state including open/close state, active terminal tracking, and recent prompts history with persistence.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/store/quickPromptStore.ts` with Zustand:
   ```typescript
   interface QuickPromptState {
     isOpen: boolean;
     activeTerminalId: string | null;
     recentPrompts: string[];  // last 10
     historyIndex: number;     // for Up/Down navigation, -1 = new input
     currentInput: string;     // draft input before history nav
   }
   ```

2. Actions:
   - `open()` - sets isOpen=true, resets historyIndex to -1
   - `close()` - sets isOpen=false
   - `toggle()` - toggles isOpen
   - `setActiveTerminal(id)` - called when terminal gains focus
   - `addToHistory(prompt)` - prepends to recentPrompts, deduplicates, caps at 10
   - `navigateHistory(direction: 'up' | 'down')` - adjusts historyIndex
   - `setCurrentInput(value)` - updates draft input

3. Persistence:
   - Persist `recentPrompts` to localStorage or `.mira/` config
   - Don't persist `isOpen` (always starts closed)

4. Integration point:
   - Subscribe to terminal focus events from terminalSessionStore (Task 9)
   - Auto-update `activeTerminalId` when user clicks/focuses a terminal panel
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: open() sets isOpen=true; addToHistory adds prompt and caps at 10; navigateHistory cycles through history correctly; duplicate prompts are deduplicated. Integration test: focus terminal → verify activeTerminalId updates.

subtasks.title: Build Input Features with History Navigation and Fuzzy Auto-Complete
subtasks.description: Implement the input functionality including Enter to send, Up/Down arrow history navigation, and fuzzy matching auto-complete dropdown from recent prompts.
subtasks.details:
1. Keyboard handlers in QuickPromptBar:
   - Enter: send prompt to active terminal PTY via WebSocket, add to history, close bar
   - Up Arrow: navigate to previous history item (preserve current input on first Up)
   - Down Arrow: navigate to next history item (or return to current input)
   - Escape: close without sending
   - Tab: accept auto-complete suggestion

2. Fuzzy auto-complete implementation:
   - Use `fuse.js` or simple fuzzy matching algorithm
   - Filter `recentPrompts` based on current input
   - Show dropdown below input with matched suggestions (max 5)
   - Highlight matching characters in suggestions
   - Arrow keys navigate suggestions when dropdown open

3. Send to terminal integration:
   - Import WebSocket client from Task 6
   - Send `pty:input` message with `activeTerminalId` and prompt text
   - Append newline character to simulate Enter press in terminal

4. History navigation UX:
   - Store original input when user starts navigating
   - Down from newest returns to original input
   - Visual indicator showing position in history (optional)
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: type 'test' → press Enter → verify pty:input message sent with correct data. Test history: add 3 prompts → press Up 3x → verify cycling through history. Test fuzzy: type 'gts' → verify 'git status' suggestion appears if in history.

subtasks.title: Implement Focus Management and Visual Feedback System
subtasks.description: Build the focus tracking and restoration system that never interrupts terminal typing, plus visual feedback for successful sends and error states when no active terminal exists.
subtasks.details:
1. Focus tracking in QuickPromptBar:
   - On open: store `document.activeElement` in ref before input focus
   - On close (send or cancel): restore focus to stored element
   - Use `requestAnimationFrame` or `setTimeout(0)` for reliable focus restoration

2. Terminal interruption prevention:
   - Check if activeElement is terminal canvas/container before opening
   - If terminal focused, store terminal panel ref for post-send focus
   - Coordinate with terminalSessionStore to ensure terminal receives focus

3. Visual feedback:
   - Success flash: brief green border/glow animation on send (200ms)
   - CSS keyframe animation for success state
   - Smooth fade-out on close

4. Error handling:
   - No active terminal state: show error message in bar "No active terminal - focus a terminal first"
   - Red border/shake animation for error state
   - Auto-dismiss error after 2 seconds OR on next open
   - Prevent send if `activeTerminalId` is null

5. Accessibility enhancements:
   - `aria-live="polite"` region for status messages
   - Screen reader announcements for success/error
   - Visible focus indicators meeting WCAG 2.1
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E test: focus terminal → Cmd+K → type 'test' → Enter → verify terminal regains focus. Test error: no terminals exist → Cmd+K → verify error message displayed. Test non-interruption: type in terminal → Cmd+K → type prompt → Enter → verify no keystroke loss in terminal.

Include visual feedback and error handling for no active terminal.

tasks.description: Create the server-side Companion AI Engine with a provider-agnostic adapter pattern supporting Claude (default), OpenAI, and Ollama. Implements context assembly and streaming responses.
tasks.details:

subtasks.description: Build the core injection flow that sends the context prompt to the target PTY via WebSocket and updates the card status to 'in-agent' with terminal reference.
subtasks.details:

   - Send formatted prompt to PTY via existing writeToSession message
   - Handle connection errors gracefully

2. Card status update flow:
   - Update `card.status` to 'in-agent'
   - Set `card.agentTarget = terminalId`
   - Store timestamp `card.sentToAgentAt`
   - Move card to 'In Progress' column if in 'Todo'

3. Zustand actions in Kanban store:
   - `sendCardToAgent(cardId, terminalId)` - orchestrates the flow
   - `clearAgentStatus(cardId)` - resets when done

4. WebSocket message format:
```typescript
{ type: 'pty:write', sessionId: string, data: string }
```

5. Handle race conditions: debounce rapid re-sends, queue if terminal busy.

   - 'error' status = agent error
6. Mira-style notifications:
   - Custom styling with companion avatar
   - Context-aware messages
7. Do not disturb mode: suppress all except errors
tasks.testStrategy: E2E test: agent completes task → notification appears within 2s. Test non-interruption: typing in terminal → notification doesn't steal focus. Test history: click icon → see past notifications.

subtasks.title: Create Notification Model and Store with CRUD Operations
subtasks.description: Define the Notification TypeScript interface and build notificationStore using Zustand with full CRUD operations, read state management, and Do Not Disturb mode support.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: addNotification creates valid notification with UUID and timestamp. markRead updates read status and decrements unreadCount. DND mode filters non-error notifications. clearAll empties array and resets unreadCount to 0.

subtasks.title: Build NotificationCenter Component with Toast Stack and History Panel
subtasks.description: Create the NotificationCenter component featuring a fixed-position toast stack for active notifications and a slide-out history panel for viewing past notifications.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/components/Notifications/NotificationCenter.tsx`:
   - Fixed position container (bottom-right by default, configurable)

2. Create `src/components/Notifications/NotificationToast.tsx`:

   - Displays title, truncated message, timestamp (relative: '2m ago')

3. Create `src/components/Notifications/NotificationHistory.tsx`:

subtasks.testStrategy: Component tests: Toast renders with correct type icon and styling. History panel slides in/out on toggle. Click on toast with terminalId triggers navigation callback. Mira-style notifications display companion avatar. Badge shows correct unread count.

subtasks.title: Implement Non-Intrusive Notification Behaviors
subtasks.description: Add focus detection to prevent notification interruption during active typing, implement slide-in animations, auto-dismiss timing, and click-to-expand functionality.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/hooks/useNotificationBehavior.ts`:
   - Track global focus state to detect active typing
   - Check if activeElement is input, textarea, or terminal (xterm.js canvas)
   - When user is typing: queue notifications instead of showing immediately
   - Flush queued notifications after 2s of inactivity
2. Implement auto-dismiss logic in NotificationToast:
   - Default 5s timeout before auto-dismiss (configurable per type)
   - Pause timer on hover (restart on mouse leave)
   - Error notifications: longer timeout (10s) or require manual dismiss
   - Clear timeout on manual dismiss
3. Create CSS animations in `src/components/Notifications/notifications.css`:
   - Slide-in from right: `transform: translateX(100%) → translateX(0)`
   - Fade-out on dismiss: opacity transition
   - Stack animation: existing toasts shift up smoothly when new one appears
4. Click-to-expand behavior:
   - Click notification → if has terminalId, emit event to focus that terminal panel
   - If no terminalId, expand to show full message in-place
5. Integrate focus detection with Quick-Prompt bar (Task 10) - don't show notifications when Quick-Prompt is open
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E test: type in terminal → trigger notification → verify notification doesn't steal focus or appear until typing stops. Test auto-dismiss: notification appears → wait 5s → verify it disappears. Test hover pause: hover notification → wait 6s → verify still visible.

subtasks.title: Integrate Agent Event Triggers from PTY Status Changes
subtasks.description: Connect the notification system to PTY status changes to automatically generate notifications when agents complete tasks or encounter errors.
subtasks.details:
1. Create `src/services/notificationTriggers.ts`:
   - Subscribe to PTY status changes via WebSocket (Task 6 dependency)
   - Listen for `pty:status` messages with status transitions
2. Define trigger rules:
   - `thinking` → `idle`: Generate 'agent-complete' notification
     - Title: 'Agent Complete' or terminal name
     - Message: 'Task finished in Terminal X' with duration if available
     - Include terminalId for click-to-navigate
   - Any status → `error`: Generate 'agent-error' notification
     - Title: 'Agent Error'
     - Message: Error details if available from PTY output
     - Always show even in DND mode
3. Add config-saved trigger:
   - Listen for config write confirmations
   - Generate 'config-saved' notification (subtle, quick auto-dismiss 3s)
4. Create context-aware message generator for Mira-style notifications:
   - Use companion persona for friendly messages
   - Example: 'I finished that task for you!' instead of 'Task complete'
   - Reference terminal by user-assigned name if available
5. Add `src/store/notificationStore.ts` subscription setup:
   - Initialize triggers on app startup
   - Cleanup subscriptions on unmount
6. Respect DND mode: error notifications bypass, all others suppressed
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: simulate PTY status change thinking→idle → verify agent-complete notification created within 2s. Test error: simulate error status → verify notification appears even in DND mode. Test Mira-style: enable Mira mode → verify friendly message format.

Include Do Not Disturb mode and Mira-style notification customization.

tasks.title: Implement MCP Connection Wizard
tasks.description: Build the conversational MCP connection wizard that allows users to connect services without editing JSON. Integrates with Mira Companion for natural language setup.
tasks.details:
1. Create `server/mcp/McpBridge.ts`:
   - Uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
   - `connect(serverConfig)` - establish MCP connection
   - `listTools()` - get available tools from server
   - `disconnect()`, `reconnect()`
2. Create `src/panels/MCP/McpStatusPanel.tsx`:
   - Grid of connection cards
   - Card shows: name, status (connected/disconnected/error), last ping, tool count
   - One-click reconnect button
3. Conversational connection flow:
   - User: "Connect my Supabase" in Companion
   - Mira: identifies service, asks for project URL
   - Mira: runs connection test silently
   - Mira: "Connected! Here's what I can do..." + tool list
4. Create `server/mcp/autoDiscover.ts`:
   - Scan project files: docker-compose.yml, .env, package.json
   - Detect services: Postgres, Redis, Supabase, Firebase, etc.
   - Surface suggestions in Companion
5. Credential handling:
   - Credentials stored in system keychain (keytar library)
   - Config stores placeholders only: `${SUPABASE_KEY}`
   - First-time setup prompts for credentials conversationally
6. MCP status updates via WebSocket
7. REST endpoints: `GET /api/mcp/status`, `POST /api/mcp/connect`
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: natural language 'connect postgres' → verify connection established. Test auto-discovery: create docker-compose with postgres → suggestions appear. Test reconnect: kill connection → verify one-click restore.

subtasks.title: Create McpBridge Core Module with SDK Integration
subtasks.description: Implement the server/mcp/McpBridge.ts class that uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk to manage MCP server connections, including connect, disconnect, reconnect, and listTools methods.
subtasks.details: Create McpBridge.ts in server/mcp/ directory using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. Implement: 1) McpConnection interface with id, name, status ('connected'|'disconnected'|'error'|'connecting'), config, lastPing, tools[], errorMessage fields. 2) connect(serverConfig: McpServerConfig) - establish connection using SDK Client, return connection object or throw typed error. 3) disconnect(connectionId: string) - gracefully close connection, clean up resources. 4) reconnect(connectionId: string) - disconnect then connect with existing config. 5) listTools(connectionId: string) - query connected server for available tools. 6) getConnection(id: string) and getAllConnections() accessors. 7) Internal connection pool Map<string, McpConnection>. 8) Connection health monitoring with configurable ping interval. Use typed errors: McpConnectionError, McpTimeoutError, McpAuthError.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: mock @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, test connect/disconnect/reconnect lifecycle, verify error handling for network failures, test listTools returns correct structure. Integration test: connect to a test MCP server, verify tools enumeration works.

subtasks.title: Implement McpStatusPanel React Component with Connection Cards
subtasks.description: Create the src/panels/MCP/McpStatusPanel.tsx panel component that displays all MCP connections as status cards with real-time status, tool counts, and one-click reconnect functionality.
subtasks.details: Create McpStatusPanel.tsx in src/panels/MCP/. Components: 1) McpStatusPanel - main panel container, subscribes to MCP state via Zustand selector, renders grid of ConnectionCard components. 2) ConnectionCard - displays: server name, status badge (color-coded: green=connected, yellow=connecting, red=error, gray=disconnected), last ping timestamp (relative time: '2m ago'), tool count badge, error message (if any). 3) Card actions: reconnect button (disabled when connecting), disconnect button, 'View Tools' expand toggle. 4) ToolsList - expandable section showing available tools with names and descriptions. 5) Empty state: 'No MCP connections configured. Say "Connect my database" to Mira.' Use CSS Grid for responsive card layout (1-3 columns based on panel width). Status updates animate smoothly (no flicker). Follow existing panel patterns from project's component architecture.
subtasks.testStrategy: Component tests: render with mock connection data, verify status badges display correctly, test reconnect button triggers action, test tool list expands/collapses. Snapshot tests for card states. E2E: verify panel receives WebSocket updates.

subtasks.title: Build Conversational Connection Flow via Companion AI Integration
subtasks.description: Implement the natural language MCP connection flow where users say things like 'Connect my Supabase' and Mira guides them through setup conversationally without any JSON editing.
subtasks.details: Create server/mcp/conversationalConnect.ts and integrate with Companion AI Engine. 1) McpIntentDetector - identifies connection requests from user messages: patterns like 'connect (my)? <service>', 'add <service> mcp', 'setup <service>'. Returns detected service type. 2) ServiceRegistry - maps service names to MCP server types and required credentials: { supabase: { type: 'supabase', requires: ['projectUrl', 'anonKey'], optional: ['serviceRoleKey'] }, postgres: { type: 'postgres', requires: ['connectionString'] }, ... }. Support: Supabase, Postgres, Redis, Firebase, GitHub, Notion, Slack. 3) ConversationFlow - state machine: detect_service → gather_credentials → test_connection → confirm_success. Each state generates Mira's response. 4) Credential gathering: Mira asks only for what she can't auto-detect, one question at a time. 5) Silent connection test before confirmation. 6) Success response includes tool list preview. 7) Error handling: retry prompts, alternative suggestions. Integrate with Companion AI Engine's message handler.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: test intent detection accuracy for various phrasings, test conversation state machine transitions. Integration test: mock LLM, verify full flow from 'connect my postgres' through to successful connection. Test error recovery flow.

subtasks.title: Create Auto-Discovery Scanner for Project Files
subtasks.description: Implement server/mcp/autoDiscover.ts that scans project configuration files (docker-compose.yml, .env, package.json) to detect services and surface MCP connection suggestions.
subtasks.details: Create autoDiscover.ts in server/mcp/. 1) ProjectScanner class with scan(projectPath: string) method. 2) File parsers: parseDockerCompose() - detect postgres, redis, mongo images with exposed ports; parseEnvFile() - detect DATABASE_URL, SUPABASE_URL, FIREBASE_*, REDIS_URL patterns; parsePackageJson() - detect @supabase/supabase-js, firebase, pg, redis, prisma dependencies. 3) ServiceDetection interface: { service: string, confidence: 'high'|'medium'|'low', source: string, detectedConfig: Partial<McpServerConfig> }. 4) Deduplication: merge detections from multiple files, prefer higher confidence. 5) scanOnProjectOpen() - triggered when project opens, caches results. 6) getSuggestions() - returns pending suggestions not yet connected or dismissed. 7) dismissSuggestion(id) - user can dismiss suggestions. Store detected services in memory, surface via Companion AI as suggestions: 'I see a Postgres container on 5432 - want to connect it?'. Suggestions appear within 5 seconds of project open per FR-MCP-03.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: parse sample docker-compose.yml with postgres/redis, verify detection. Test .env parsing with various URL patterns. Test deduplication logic. Integration test: create temp project with config files, verify suggestions surfaced.

subtasks.title: Implement System Keychain Integration for Credential Storage
subtasks.description: Create secure credential storage using keytar library to store MCP credentials in the system keychain, ensuring secrets never appear in .mira/ config files.
subtasks.details: Create server/credentials/KeychainStore.ts using keytar library. 1) KeychainStore class: setCredential(service: string, account: string, credential: string), getCredential(service: string, account: string), deleteCredential(service: string, account: string), hasCredential(service: string, account: string). 2) Service naming convention: 'mira-studio-<project-hash>-<mcp-service-name>'. 3) CredentialPlaceholder system: config stores '${SUPABASE_KEY}' placeholder, runtime resolves from keychain. 4) resolveCredentials(config: McpServerConfig) - replaces placeholders with actual values. 5) storeFromConfig(config: McpServerConfig) - extracts and stores credentials, returns config with placeholders. 6) Error handling: KeychainAccessDenied, CredentialNotFound errors. 7) Platform support: macOS Keychain, Linux Secret Service, Windows Credential Manager (via keytar's cross-platform support). 8) First-time setup detection: if placeholder exists but keychain entry missing, trigger credential prompt flow. Never log or expose actual credential values.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: mock keytar, test store/retrieve/delete cycle, verify placeholder resolution. Test error handling when keychain access denied. Integration test: actually store and retrieve on dev machine (manual). Test credential migration when project cloned.

subtasks.title: Implement MCP Status WebSocket Updates
subtasks.description: Create WebSocket infrastructure to push real-time MCP connection status updates to the frontend, ensuring UI stays synchronized with actual connection states.
subtasks.details: Create server/mcp/McpStatusBroadcaster.ts. 1) Extend existing WebSocket infrastructure (from PTY Manager patterns). 2) McpStatusMessage types: { type: 'mcp:status', connectionId: string, status: ConnectionStatus, tools?: Tool[], error?: string }, { type: 'mcp:connected', connectionId: string, tools: Tool[] }, { type: 'mcp:disconnected', connectionId: string, reason?: string }, { type: 'mcp:error', connectionId: string, error: string }, { type: 'mcp:ping', connectionId: string, latency: number }. 3) McpBridge emits events on state changes, McpStatusBroadcaster listens and broadcasts to connected clients. 4) Client-side: useMcpStatus() hook that subscribes to WebSocket updates and maintains Zustand state. 5) Initial sync: on WebSocket connect, send current state of all connections. 6) Heartbeat: periodic ping messages to verify connection health (every 30s). 7) Reconnection handling: when WebSocket reconnects, resync full state. Status updates within 1 second per NFR targets.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: verify message serialization, test broadcaster sends on McpBridge events. Integration test: connect WebSocket client, change connection status, verify client receives update within 1 second. Test reconnection sync.

subtasks.title: Create REST Endpoints for MCP Operations
subtasks.description: Implement REST API endpoints for MCP operations: GET /api/mcp/status for connection status, POST /api/mcp/connect for initiating connections, and related management endpoints.
subtasks.details: Create server/routes/mcp.ts with Express/Fastify route handlers. Endpoints: 1) GET /api/mcp/status - returns all connections with status, tools, lastPing. Response: { connections: McpConnection[] }. 2) GET /api/mcp/status/:id - single connection details. 3) POST /api/mcp/connect - body: { service: string, config: Partial<McpServerConfig> }. Stores credentials in keychain, attempts connection, returns result. 4) POST /api/mcp/disconnect/:id - disconnect specific connection. 5) POST /api/mcp/reconnect/:id - reconnect specific connection. 6) GET /api/mcp/tools/:id - list tools for connection. 7) POST /api/mcp/test - test connection without persisting. 8) GET /api/mcp/suggestions - returns auto-discovered service suggestions. 9) POST /api/mcp/suggestions/:id/dismiss - dismiss suggestion. Request validation with Zod schemas. Proper error responses with typed error codes. Rate limiting on connect endpoints (prevent spam). Authentication via session (integrate with existing auth patterns).
subtasks.testStrategy: API tests: test each endpoint with valid/invalid inputs, verify response schemas. Test error responses return correct status codes. Integration test: POST /api/mcp/connect with valid config, verify connection established and appears in GET /api/mcp/status.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the MCP Connection Wizard into: 1) McpBridge class using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk with connect, listTools, disconnect methods, 2) McpStatusPanel with connection cards showing status, tools, reconnect, 3) Conversational connection flow via Companion AI integration, 4) Auto-discovery scanner for project files (docker-compose, .env, package.json), 5) System keychain integration for credential storage (keytar), 6) MCP status WebSocket updates, 7) REST endpoints for MCP operations. No JSON editing requirement.

tasks.title: Build Skill System Runtime
tasks.description: Implement the Skill System runtime that loads, validates, and executes skill manifests. Skills run in a sandboxed context with declared permissions only.

subtasks.title: Define SkillManifest TypeScript Interface and Types
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive TypeScript types for skill manifests including all declared fields: cornerstones, panels, permissions, agent_injections, claude_md_additions, wizard_steps, keybindings, composes_with, and conflicts_with arrays.
subtasks.details:
Create `src/types/skill.ts` with:

1. **Core SkillManifest interface**:
   - `id: string` - unique skill identifier
   - `name: string` - display name
   - `version: string` - semver version
   - `cornerstones: Cornerstone[]` - mapped to the five lifecycle phases
   - `permissions: Permission[]` - declared API access rights

2. **Panel definitions**: `PanelDefinition` type with `id`, `type`, `component`, `defaultPosition`

4. **Wizard step types**: `WizardStep` with `question`, `type`, `options`, `dependsOn`
